title: performance: container restore --import decompresses entire snapshot for flag parsing
body:
### Issue Description

See cmd/podman/root.go:259, which does

```
		// To make it easier for users we will look into the checkpoint archive and
		// set the runtime to the one used during checkpointing.
...

			if cmd.Flag("import").Changed {
				runtime, err := crutils.CRGetRuntimeFromArchive(cmd.Flag("import").Value.String())
```



CRGetRuntimeFromArchive uses the archive.Untar functionality to extract a single file from the snapshot, using `Exclude` to skip over entries. 

archive.Untar skips over entries using `tar.Reader.Next`. 

The `tar` package attempts to seek to avoid reading, but this only works for seekable inputs (ie. `*os.File`). Because of the vagaries of the archive package, the input stream, -even if it is uncompressed- is wrapped in a 32kb buffer (see `archive.DecompressStream`), which is not seekable.

After the config.dump and spec.dump files were extracted, Untar still has to read the entire archive, as `archive.Untar` has no way of knowing that there are no other files matching the Include/Exclude specs.

In my use case, it's common to have large (10-100 Gb) snapshots, so this extremely expensive.

In general, the tar file format is probably not a good file format for storing these data. Have you considered zip instead?

comments.body:
Also, to create the container, the above anti-pattern is repeated, stack trace:



A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
